summary: Read-only gameplay research concluded that M7 client work must change direction: current schema/topology surfaces are sufficient to proceed, but the client default snapshot/read model still risks old M2 grid projection. The client must consume topology-backed district polygons, route nodes, route edges, path previews, and reason codes instead of disguising grid or centroid logic.

decisions.decision: This is not BLOCKED_FOR_SCHEMA_GAP because accepted schema/query surfaces exist, including map topology listing, topology path preview, topology hash, edge/path reason codes, and fixture anti-grid cases.

Use ADR-010 hybrid topology: district/province polygons are the governance and selection surface; explicit route/transport graph edges are the movement, supply, obligation delivery, campaign, AI, and reason-code surface.

The client must add topology-facing projections inside allowed client paths; if existing read models cannot express required route edge/path details, report an allowed-scope gap rather than inventing renderer-side authority.
Nobunaga-like and Total War-like campaign maps communicate castles/settlements, regions, roads, route capacity, provisions, and campaign targets; they are not regular hidden-grid tile boards.

risks.summary: packages/client-core/src/index.ts still contains M2_PROTOTYPE_GRID_COLUMNS and center/anchor-derived legacy projections that must not drive the player-facing default map.
packages/map-renderer/src/index.ts still has anchor/centroid fallback behaviors; route rendering must prefer explicit topology route-node polylines and edge sequences.

A map can look non-rectangular while still behaving like a grid; acceptance must include visual-neighbor-without-route, visually-distant-explicit-route, and geometry-perturbation-without-path-change evidence.

requested_action: Forward hard anti-grid constraints to the client_engineer. Require topology-backed client projection, route preview, renderer plan, localized reason copy, screenshots, and anti-grid tests before REVIEW.
